name: 1799 Prime

notes: Every frozen row was reconciled against the current restaurant-issued July 2026 dinner menu and its visually rendered pages.
The corrected represented surface contains 68 current food items and 18 nonalcoholic beverages; beverage sections are ordered after food and dessert.
Alcohol-only wine and cocktail pages remain outside this allergen-focused menu surface, consistent with the existing food/nonalcoholic extraction scope.
Direct contains signals are derived only from item names and descriptions in the current official menu.

The menu explicitly warns that GF items may contact gluten in the shared kitchen and does not guarantee against gluten cross-contact; GF rows therefore carry mayContain gluten rather than a negative claim.
Variable items such as Soup Du Jour, Fresh Catch, Featured Entree, and Featured Dessert remain allergen-data unavailable unless their current description directly identifies an allergen.
The baseline used an older dinner PDF, mixed website navigation and dress-code text into menu rows, collapsed side dishes and modifier groups, and retained dishes absent from the July 2026 menu.
The prior extraction dropped the GF cross-contact warning and produced 23 incorrect or incomplete allergen rows among 36 surviving menu matches.

findings.summary: Seventeen of 53 frozen rows were stale dishes or non-items, including navigation, dress-code text, protein modifiers, and collapsed side groups.
findings.resolution: Replaced the stale output with 86 current food/nonalcoholic rows and represented every side as its own item.

findings.summary: Twenty-three of 36 surviving baseline rows had incorrect contains, may-contain, or source semantics.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt signals from current item text, removed adjacent-row bleed, and left variable or undisclosed items unavailable.

findings.summary: The baseline treated GF labeling without preserving the restaurant’s explicit warning that GF foods may contact gluten.
findings.resolution: Mapped the warning to mayContain gluten on every GF item and every Butcher Block selection covered by the section-level GF statement.

findings.summary: The baseline referenced Dinner Menu Revamp 6th Edit while the official site now publishes Dinner Menu July 2026.
findings.resolution: Pinned the correction to the current official July 2026 PDF with retained hash and artifact.

repairs.summary: Prepared and targeted-tested an 86-item current snapshot with individual side rows, beverages last, direct allergen signals, and GF gluten cross-contact semantics.
verificationCommands: RESTAURANT_REPOSITORY_PATH=tmp/restaurant-verification/1799-prime-repair-input.json node scripts/repair-final-generated-regressions.mjs (targeted copy; verified 86 items, 49 direct disclosures, 16 cross-contact-only rows, 21 unavailable rows, and zero known artifacts)

notes: The July PDF pages 4-9 were rendered to PNG and visually reviewed to verify column and section boundaries before catalog construction.
